title: Retrieve Tote Exchange Settings
summary: Use the Get Exchange Settings function on the tote management API page to retrieve tote exchange settings and configuration information for review or documentation.
when_to_use: Use when you need to retrieve and review the current tote exchange settings and configuration information from the tote management interface/API page.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to modify tote exchange settings.
Do not use this procedure to infer setting meanings or corrective actions beyond what the source provides.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the tote management interface or API-backed Get Exchange Settings function, retrieve the settings, review the returned values, and record them exactly as shown.

safety_notes: Do not invent setting meanings or corrective actions beyond what the source provides.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to tote management interface or API-backed Get Exchange Settings function

access_required: Access to tote management interface or API-backed Get Exchange Settings function
system_preconditions: The Get Exchange Settings function is available on the tote management API page.

visual_references.description: Training slide/frame showing the tote management API page with Get Exchange Settings listed among tote management functions.

steps.title: Open Get Exchange Settings
steps.purpose: Navigate to the tote management function used to retrieve exchange settings.
steps.instruction: Open the tote management API page and locate the Get Exchange Settings function.
steps.expected_result: The Get Exchange Settings function is visible and available to use.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote management API page shows Get Exchange Settings as an available function.
steps.failure_condition: The Get Exchange Settings function cannot be found or accessed.

actions.label: Open tote management function
actions.exact_action: Open the Get Exchange Settings function.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API page listing Get Exchange Settings among the available tote management functions.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if exchange settings cannot be retrieved.
Stop if the Get Exchange Settings function is not available in the interface shown by the source.

source_evidence.text: Open the Get Exchange Settings function. [Image support needed: training slide or interface view showing the Get Exchange Settings function in the tote management API page.]

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Get Exchange Settings — Retrieves settings and configurations for tote exchanges.
Visible functions include Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.

steps.title: Run settings retrieval
steps.purpose: Execute the retrieval function so the current exchange settings are returned.
steps.instruction: Run the retrieval action for exchange settings from the Get Exchange Settings function.
steps.expected_result: The interface returns tote exchange settings and configuration information.
steps.healthy_condition: Settings and configuration information are returned by the function.
steps.failure_condition: No settings are returned, or the retrieval cannot be completed.

actions.label: Retrieve exchange settings
actions.exact_action: Run the retrieval action for exchange settings.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Get Exchange Settings function on the tote management API page that is used to retrieve settings.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if exchange settings cannot be retrieved.

source_evidence.text: Run the retrieval action for exchange settings.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Get Exchange Settings — Retrieves settings and configurations for tote exchanges.

steps.title: Review returned settings
steps.purpose: Inspect the retrieved output to understand what settings and configuration values are currently shown.
steps.instruction: Review the returned tote exchange settings and configuration values.
steps.expected_result: The returned settings are visible for inspection.
steps.healthy_condition: The interface displays tote exchange settings and configuration values clearly enough to review.
steps.failure_condition: Returned values are not visible, incomplete, or cannot be reviewed.

actions.label: Inspect returned values
actions.exact_action: Review the returned tote exchange settings and configuration values.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if exchange settings cannot be retrieved.

source_evidence.text: Review the returned tote exchange settings and configuration values.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Retrieves settings and configurations for tote exchanges.

steps.title: Compare settings to intended interpretation
steps.purpose: Use the retrieved values as a reference for the exchange action you are trying to interpret or document.
steps.instruction: Compare the displayed settings to the exchange action you plan to interpret or document.
steps.expected_result: The user has aligned the displayed settings with the exchange action under review.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed settings can be referenced directly for interpretation or documentation.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed settings do not provide enough information to support the intended interpretation or documentation.

actions.label: Compare displayed settings
actions.exact_action: Compare the displayed settings to the exchange action you plan to interpret or document.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if interpretation would require inventing setting meanings not provided by the source.

source_evidence.text: Compare the displayed settings to the exchange action you plan to interpret or document.
This is a procedural reference candidate because the source only supports retrieval and interpretation of settings, not modification.

steps.title: Record settings exactly as shown
steps.purpose: Preserve the retrieved settings accurately for support reference or documentation.
steps.instruction: Record the observed settings exactly as returned by the interface.
steps.expected_result: The observed settings are documented exactly as displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: Recorded values match the returned interface values without added interpretation.
steps.failure_condition: Recorded values are incomplete, altered, or include unsupported interpretation.

actions.label: Document returned settings
actions.exact_action: Record the observed settings exactly as returned by the interface.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if you would need to add unsupported meaning or corrective guidance beyond what the source provides.

source_evidence.text: Record the observed settings exactly as returned by the interface.
Do not invent setting meanings or corrective actions beyond what the source provides.

success_criteria: The Get Exchange Settings function is accessed successfully.
Tote exchange settings and configuration information are retrieved.
The returned settings are reviewed and recorded exactly as displayed.
healthy_conditions: Get Exchange Settings is visible on the tote management API page.
The retrieval returns settings and configuration information for tote exchanges.
Observed settings can be reviewed and documented without adding unsupported interpretation.
failure_conditions: The Get Exchange Settings function cannot be found or accessed.
The user would need to invent setting meanings or corrective actions not supported by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if exchange settings cannot be retrieved.
Escalate if the interface does not provide the expected settings and configuration information.
Do not provide corrective actions or setting interpretations beyond what the source explicitly supports.
